comment: Beer #1 is clear copper. A creamy moussy head. Don't know if it's the foamy cap or what but I fail to get any olfactory nuance at all. Completely numb. The taste is reasonable, yet not very challenging: malt, biscuit, orange pith, faint resin and grass.

beer_style: IPA - Imperial / Double New England / Hazy

comment: Beer #2 is basically clear, with a faint haze only, and colors amber. Wheat malt, biscuit and orange peel in the scent. Very much the same cocktail on the tongue, as well: wheat malt, biscuit and orange peel. Adding lemon and a yeasty touch though.

comment: Beer #1 is dressed in relatively transparent amber. The scent is surprisingly weak: a hint of grapefruit only. The taste compensates the inadequacy: grapefruit zest and pith, lemon, orange peel, pine needle, a hint of resin and a biscuity idea.

Beer #2 is a tad cloudy, deep gold. The fragrance is awkward: greasy sausage, mixed with distant solvent. Taste: greasy grilled sausage, lemon zest, faint butter and a suggestion of wheat malt. No rating as this seems to be spoiled.

Murky, deep dark brown beer, like Coca-Cola. Nose: baked dark bread that is relatively shy. Taste: sweet rye malt, brown bread, brown sugar, a bit of syrup, Coca-Cola and a whisper of orange peel. Light, soft-drink-like, lip-glueing, soft and smooth.

comment: A nice burger joint in the old Market Hall. Transparent, dark brown beer. Dark malt, molasses and a suggestion of cacao nibs can be found in the scent. Dark roasted malt, molasses, bitter dark caramel and dark bread constitute the gustatory cocktail.

Semiclear gold beer. Wheat malt and lemon zest in the scent. The taste is more citrusy than what the fragrance suggests: lemon zest as well as grapefruit peel and pith plus wheat malt and grassy dry hops. Feels light, zesty, faintly crisp, refreshing, dry

Lucid, dark amber–brown beer. A syrupy scent. Sweet skärgårdsbröd and brown sugar escort the chief. The taste is really sweet, brown-sugary, caramelly and syrupy. The mouthfeel is light, sappy, soft, smooth, soft-drink-like and modestly sparkling.

beer_name: HOP SERIES: HAZY IPA Mosaic, Nelson Sauvin, Mandarina Bavaria

comment: Clear amber cider with sparkling carbonation and a rushed head. Medium-sour apple mash finds my nose. And citric acid. The apple zest is nicely sour-leaning on the tastebuds. Citric acid follows especially in the end. Feels light, acidic, juicy and pulpy.

Lucid, deep copper kaimiškas. A grayish beige head. The nose receives strong fermented banana, a good honey slam, waffle and apricot but no rye in any form. The tongue likes the generous honey, fermented banana, pancake, biscuit and a wink of apricot.
Opaque black beer. Nose: cacao nibs, raw chocolate, roasted malt and as if a dash of cinnamon. Taste: roasted malt, raw cacao powder, raw chocolate and distant cookie.

Flight 1/3 is translucent, deep gold. A white velvety head that reaches one finger. No scent... nothing. Lemon zest, lime and orange pith land on my tongue. And a twist of pale malt. Feels light minus, zesty, pulpy and slightly drying.

Flight 2/3 is a tad misty, strawberry red. A clean white lacing. Medium-sweet cherry juice in the air. Still, a sugary addition joins the show. Cherry juice also on the tongue, both a bit sweet and modestely sour. The mouthfeel is light, juicy and summery

Flight 3/3 is lucid gold. The olfactory supply gives grapefruit zest and peel as well as a hint of grass. Grapefruit dances also on the tastebuds, both zest and pith. Wheat malt, lemon pulp and a whisper of grass, as well. Light, zesty, a tad dry, drying.

Clear, orange, honey infused looking beer. Nose: fresh mango and passionfruit. The same phenomenon can be observed on the tongue: passionfruit is equal with mango. Wheat malt and a hint of biscuit in the background. Light, juicy, pulpy and tropical.

Korean food for a late dinner. Clear amber beer with a subdued head. Sweet-leaning pale malt and biscuit in the air. The same cocktail on the tongue. A faint hoppy tail. Feels light, lip-glueing, bulky and industrial. Does the job, nothing more.

Transparent straw beer. A dense moussy white head on the top. Wheat biscuit and orange peel in the scent. Orange grows on the tongue, followed by wheat malt and biscuit. The sensation is thin, zesty, spiced and refreshing.
brewery_name: Brick by Brick (Raudonų Plytų Alaus Dirbtuvės)
